title: Cabinet Door Organizer with Paper Towel Holder
summary: A small shelved organizer that mounts to the inside of a kitchen cabinet door on L-brackets — curved-top sides, two rail supports, and an optional paper towel holder or top shelf.
description:
This organizer turns dead space on the inside of a cabinet door into real storage — a small shelved box, mounted with L-brackets so it hangs on the door rather than taking up shelf space inside the cabinet. Attach the bottom to the sides first with whatever fastening method you prefer, then add the rail supports at the top and bottom for extra rigidity — a genuinely simple three-piece assembly before any decorative shaping happens.

The curved top cuts on the side pieces are entirely optional and can be done at any point in the build — before assembly if you want to clamp the pieces flat on a bench, or after if you'd rather see the finished shape before committing to the jigsaw cut. Either order works structurally.

The top opening is genuinely flexible: add a paper towel holder rod for the classic version, or skip it and add a plain shelf instead if you'd rather store spice jars or cleaning supplies. **Mount with L-brackets and hardware rated for the door's actual thickness** — an overloaded or poorly mounted door organizer swinging on a hinge is a real fall hazard for anything loaded inside it.

materials.name: Brad nails, 2", or trim screws, or pocket hole screws, 1-1/4"
Small L brackets with 1/2 in hardware
Primer and paint, or stain and poly

steps.title: Build the box
steps.body: Attach the bottom to the sides using your preferred fastening method and glue. The curved top cuts can be done now or later, whichever is easier for you.
materials: Brad nails, 2", or trim screws, or pocket hole screws, 1-1/4"

steps.title: Add the rails
steps.body: Add rails to the top and bottom for extra support. Add a paper towel holder rod at the top, or a plain shelf if you'd rather store other items.
materials: Brad nails, 2", or trim screws, or pocket hole screws, 1-1/4"

steps.title: Shape the sides (if not done already)
steps.body: Cut the curved top shape into the side pieces with a jigsaw if you haven't already.

steps.title: Mount and finish
steps.body: Sand and finish. Attach L brackets rated for your cabinet door's thickness and mount the organizer to the inside of the door.
materials: Small L brackets with 1/2 in hardware
Primer and paint, or stain and poly

unresolvedImages.alt: Small organizer mounted to the inside of a kitchen cabinet door
